name: dev-rfc-review-ui
prompt: I've got this skill at skills/.experimental/dev-rfc/ that generates an HTML review page for RFC documents. The review template at assets/review_template.html renders user-submitted RFC markdown and displays it in the browser. Can you do a security analysis of this skill? I want to understand what could go wrong.
expected_output: A structured threat model identifying the XSS in review_template.html, trust boundary classification, and context-aware calibration.
assertions: Output has all 4 sections: Overview, Trust boundaries, Attack surfaces, Criticality calibration
Trust boundaries section classifies inputs into at least 2 distinct tiers (attacker-controlled vs developer-controlled)
Output identifies marked.parse or markdown rendering into innerHTML without sanitization as a risk
Output references the specific file review_template.html in an attack surface section
At least one attack surface includes a concrete attacker story with action and impact
Criticality section acknowledges that the review UI runs on localhost, affecting severity
